widgets_values:
When cfg=1, the negative prompt is ignored.
Using NAG can be helpful.

nodes.title: Dev full
widgets_values:
In the dev full, only sampler2 uses the distilled LoRA.

Also, you need to replace sampler1 with this >>>>>>>>

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (negative Prompt)
widgets_values: blurry, low quality, still frame, frames, watermark, overlay, titles, has blurbox, has subtitles

nodes.title: control data
widgets_values:
lora: canny, depth, pose
https://huggingface.co/collections/Lightricks/ltx-2

You can use Canny, depth, and pose as inputs along with LoRA.

Input control data into both samplers will improve quality, but it will consume a lot of resources. 
For control data, it is recommended to disconnect the node connection and turn off the insert to Sampler2.

Also, it should be used a loader other than the first one, and the strength value should be set to 1.

(If you input to both samplers, both samplers must use the LoRA)

nodes.title: Dev+destilled lora
widgets_values:
distilled-lora: https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/blob/main/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors

Here, the Dev version allows you to apply the distilled LoRA separately for each sampler, 
or you can apply it together in the previous node.

nodes.title: Image
widgets_values:
If you turn off all image, it's T2V.

frame_idx is the position where the image will be inserted.
higher image_compression values result in more motion.
strength=1: The image will be applied as is.
strength=0: The image will be ignored.

Except for the first image, the image aspect ratio is not maintained.
If the image uses a different aspect ratio than the video, do not bypass this upscale.

For video extension, inputting the images into the first loader can save resources.

If you have 2xframe turned on, you will need to double the frame_idx in the image input for sampler2.

nodes.title: Audio
widgets_values:
The loaded audio is trimmed from start_index(second) to a length of duration(second), and then inserted at frame_idx.

placing an empty exemple.mp3 file in the ComfyUI\input folder will prevent errors.
If vae decode automatically switches to tiling, turn on the tiling switch.

nodes.title: Sampler
widgets_values:
This is a 2.0 setting, but it works fine. 

Similar setup to the official WF are below.
sampler1 euler_a_cfg_pp, linear_quadratic scheduler.
sampler2 euler_cfg_pp, beta scheduler, denoise 0.37~0.38 
but it takes twice as long!

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
